It's not about the ability to take the island. It's the logistics, the number of Patriot systems you'd need to divert to that area just to defend your troops, while Iran would resume bombing the Gulf countries... all for nothing, since they can't move from there and are within artillery range. After the ceasefire, Iran has been able to regroup; it would require a few more days of suppression. The occupation of Kjarg will only happen if they plan a deeper campaign in Iran, probably seeking to control the entire coast, to try to secure control of the strait. However, this would require a much larger escalation, as well as mobilizing more troops and resources.
